reason: All four findings are minor in nature. The omission in s3 lacks contextual detail but the implementation guidance (allowDoubleSubmission=false) is fully present. The fabricated OutOfMemoryError hint is incorrect metadata but does not corrupt the section body. The two hints_missing findings concern secondary or common framework classes unlikely to be primary search targets. No finding impairs a user's ability to correctly implement the described feature.

findings_assessment.location: s3 (JavaScriptを使用した二重サブミットの防止)
findings_assessment.justification: The s3 section body already states that double submission causes duplicate DB operations and provides the implementation method (allowDoubleSubmission=false). The missing trigger-condition context (double-click / repeated click when no response) is supplementary background that helps distinguish JS vs token approaches, but the distinction itself is inferrable from reading both s3 and s4. An agent can guide a user to the correct implementation without this detail. Phase E did not fix this because the existing content is functionally sufficient and the omission is purely contextual enrichment.

OutOfMemoryError is a fabricated hint — the source only mentions 「メモリ不足」. A spurious hint can cause false-positive routing (a user searching OutOfMemoryError might land on s7 unnecessarily), but the s7 section body accurately uses 「メモリ不足」 and does not mention OutOfMemoryError. No misinformation is delivered once the section is read. Phase E did not remove this hint, likely judging that an extra routing signal with no body-level falsehood does not warrant a fix pass.

UsersEntity and UgroupSystemAccountEntity are parameter types in the registerUser method signature. They are not the subject of s7 (which teaches ParameterizedSqlPStatement, addBatchObject, executeBatch etc.) and are unlikely to be direct user search terms in this context. Their absence reduces routing precision marginally but does not prevent correct implementation guidance. The code sample already shows these classes in full. Phase E left them unfixed, consistent with a low-impact judgment on missing secondary class hints.

HttpResponse, HttpRequest, and ExecutionContext are pervasive Nablarch framework classes that appear across many knowledge files. Their absence from s8 hints is a minor routing gap: users searching specifically for these classes may not land on s8, but the classes themselves are not the focus of s8 (which teaches @OnDoubleSubmission, re-validation, and token-check flow). The full method signature including these types is present in the s8 code sample. Phase E did not add them, consistent with low-priority treatment of common cross-file framework class hints.
